direction: Direction 0: workspace-JIT baseline clone (parity + correctness gate)
lineage: faithful clone of sglang python/sglang/jit_kernel/csrc/diffusion/qknorm_rope.cuh @ c47f0e7cd (one-warp-per-(token,head), 256 thr/CTA, FP32 RMS accum, 128-bit vec)
build: sglang load_jit/tvm-ffi; cuda_files via ../-relpath to task-owned .cuh + extra_include_paths=src/csrc; flags = sglang default (-std=c++20 -O3 --expt-relaxed-constexpr -DSGL_CUDA_ARCH=900); NO --use_fast_math; NO torch.cpp_extension; NO EXPORTS
dispatch_gate: bf16 + cuda + contiguous + 16B-aligned + non-aliased q/k + head_dim in {64,128,256} + rope_dim gate
evidence_note: build_smoke=parity vs upstream(0)+oracle(0.0625); correctness_nogrid=79 passed; correctness_grid=144 passed (NOTE: this 144 was the clone; the warp2 grid re-validation is correctness_round1.log under d3-final)

change: src/wrapper.py: cached static gate (_static_eligible); dropped per-call untyped_storage aliasing probe + 3 of 5 data_ptr%16 alignment probes (allocator-guaranteed for contiguous CUDA tensors); cheap q.data_ptr()!=k.data_ptr() aliasing

benchmark.note: kernel == baseline (module ~1.0x); win is leaner Python dispatch than upstream

status: KEPT then refined in d3 (cache_once); wrapper-level dispatch win

change: added fused_qknorm_rope_warp2: 16 lanes/head, 8 bf16/lane via one 128-bit load, 16-lane RMS reduce (shfl_xor per-half mask), GPT-J rope half_idx=lane_in_head*4+i/2, per-half-warp q/k decode. benchmark.note: HISTORICAL: lru_cache wrapper; superseded by d3-final (cache_once + rope_dim gate) at 1.0723x/1.0268x

direction: Final: REAL in-SGLang .cuh placement + replacement test (AC-13)
